response.justification: Candidate is a solid senior/staff IC with broad polyglot experience, but the role requires a Director-level Principal Engineer with 15+ years and recognized AI expertise. Resume shows ~14-16 years of mostly product/web engineering with only recent, peripheral AI exposure (MCP tooling at Google since April 2025) and no demonstrated AI products, platforms, or strategic advisory work with executives or strategic customers. Employment entries also raise credibility concerns (overlapping 'Founder, Amazon' with full-time roles elsewhere, which reads inconsistently).
strengths: Broad full-stack and polyglot engineering background across web, mobile, embedded, and high-throughput systems
Strong software craftsmanship signal: TDD, CI/CD, OSS contributions, workshops, and public talks
Recent exposure to AI-agent infrastructure and MCP tooling at Google
concerns: No demonstrated AI/ML products, platforms, or research — preferred AI expertise is largely absent
Title trajectory (Senior/Staff) is below Director-level Principal IC scope; no evidence of advising strategic customers or shaping cloud/AI strategy
Overlapping concurrent roles (e.g., 'Founder, Amazon' running in parallel with Meta/RIDE/Google) and unclear FAANG role framing reduce credibility

key_factors.factor: Lack of recognized AI expertise / AI products built
Seniority mismatch vs. Director-level Principal IC scope
Strong generalist engineering breadth and craftsmanship
